ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deena Zacharin for and with Maurice Ward.

Ms. Zacharin stated that she received the notes from the Probate Examiner, that Mr. Ward's fourth accounting increased by $36,000.00 in income of the trust, that Mr. Ward worked hard to ready the property for rental, that he fixed the property, that the rent went from $1,800.00 to $2,900.00 a month, and that he has preserved and maintained the property. Ms. Zacharin also stated that Ms. Ward lives with her Daughter, that the trust does not pay rent to the Daughter, utilities or for most of the food, that occasionally the Daughter does ask for reimbursement, that Shawna is the conservator of the person and that she believes there was a conservatorship of the estate early on, but Counsel at that time created this trust under a motion for summary judgment. In addition, Ms. Zacharin stated that the Petitioner feels $2,000.00 in automobile expenses in two years is reasonable, that the return check fee was never an issue, that there were attorney fees paid, that he did not realize that he needed to move money over, that the expense of $200.00 was for Jacqueline to buy Christmas gifts for the children, and that Jacqueline was fine with helping out the Daughter and Granddaughter with $130.00. Furthermore, Ms. Zacharin stated that Mr. Ward was finalizing the property for rental, that he did some of the work (painting/back yard) himself, that he charged ten hours for the whole day which she thought was reasonable, and that if the Court will not allow it, she prefers the Court take it out of her fees, that Mr. Watson is a Realtor, that he showed the place for a month and a half, that he did background checks, screened tenants, that generally people charge a months rent for this service, but they got a good rate and a good tenant from him, that Mr. Watson is not related to the Petitioner and that she does not know if Shawna has been paid. The accounting petition is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-04-2019 Continued To Mar-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deena Zacharin, Esq. for Maurice Ward.

Ms. Zacharin stated that she was here to address why there wasn't a response to the examiner notes, that the fourth account was filed at the end of August, that it was given a January hearing date, and that the January hearing date was then continued to February 4, 2019. Ms. Zacharin also stated that she was working with her client and his Son on 28 different points, that she had asked for a 30-day extension, that she had a 9 page declaration ready for review by her client, that her client's 82 year old brother was hospitalized, and that due to the hospitalization her client was unable to review/sign the declaration. In closing, Ms. Zacharin stated that a few days later she received notes from the Probate Examiner that the matter was on for hearing for failing to respond to the examiner notes, that she intended on requesting a continunace, that the declaration is prepared, that it needs to be reviewed and signed by her client, that it appeared that the Probate Examiner had reduced the number of questions in the notes, and that she would delete that information now. Ms. Zacharin presented a letter from her client in open court regarding the hospitalization. The Court continued the matter to March 25, 2019, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deena Zacharin for and with Maurice Ward (Successor Trustee).
Will Doyle specially appearing for Bruce Feder/Former Counsel for Mr. Ward.
Shawna Suazo (Conservator of the person of Jacqueline Ward) also present.

The Parties explained services provided and repairs done (mold abatement, carpeting, plumbing, painting, patching of wholes, etc.) to the property to address the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes with respect to the Trustee's fees, repair fees exceeding the amount authorized by the Court's order, the Organizer's fees and the Attorney's fees. Ms. Zacharin also explained that the Trustee did not request fees for two years, that the property is not rented out, but is on the market, that they may have to lower the asking rent amount, and that one of the units can't be rented out for one more year because the tenants vacated for the purpose of Ms. Ward moving back in (she moved in, but had to move out due to health issues). Mr. Ward stated that the backyard needs work, that records are now being kept and that things should be in order for the next accounting. Ms. Suazo reported that the Conservatee is doing well and is now living with her. The Third Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deena Zacharin for and with (trustee) Maurice Ward.

Ms. Zacharin reported that she has changed firms and has substituted into this case, that the property had been fully rented, that one couple left and the other tenants were asked to leave, that they made repairs on one unit and Jacqueline is living in it, that the other unit needs repairs for possible mold issues and repair of drywalls, repair expenses estimated at $25,000.00 for painting, drywall repair and to find the cause of the dry rot, once the repairs a complete they are looking at Jacqueline living in one unit and her daughter living in the other unit, rent free, to care for her mother. Ms. Zacharin reported on the disclosure of relatives issue stating that Shaunice is a granddauther and is being paid $200.00 per month to help, that the son-in-law was paid $500.00 to do some emergency plumbing and that they did not see these two as agents, that Mr. Ward is Jacqueline's ex husband who is trying to help and not charging fees, that the $300.00 per month paid to Shaunice was an error, that Shaunice kept records of the work she did on her calendar and that it has been confirmed that she provided the services, that Jacqueline is blind and that Shaunice will live rent free in exchange for cooking, cleaning, etc. In regards to fees Ms. Zacharin stated that the payment of $8515.00 was a relocation fee to the tenants, that the $1500.00 fee to the Kato, Feder firm was an advance of costs, that $450.00 was used for a filing fee, that $1050.00 is still in the trust account at the Kato, Feder firm and will go against the fees and that Maurice Ward is not taking fees and is doing a tremendous amount of work. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
